basis: CIF NWE / Dated Brent physical proxy
note: Physical crude still trades above Brent futures, but the post-deal premium has eased sharply as the US–Iran memorandum reduces immediate Hormuz closure risk. Trade-press reporting now points to de-escalation rather than a renewed war premium: paper crude has fallen back toward the high-$70s/low-$80s, while physical North West European barrels retain only a reduced prompt-delivery premium because tanker flows, insurance confidence and Gulf export logistics have not fully normalised.
